expectations.evidence: user.query.ts imports LoadStateAggregateClient and SnapshotQueryClient from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-wow (lines 8-10)

expectations.text: Command includes proper method (POST) and body structure for creating user
expectations.evidence: user.command.ts uses HttpMethod.POST with body containing name and email

expectations.text: Query includes condition builder (eq) for fetching by ID
expectations.evidence: user.query.ts uses load(id) directly rather than a condition builder like eq() or aggregateId(). The LoadStateAggregateClient.load() method takes ID directly rather than using a QueryDSL condition builder.

expectations.text: Includes proper TypeScript typing for command request and result
expectations.evidence: user.command.ts has CreateUserCommandBody interface and CommandRequest<CreateUserCommandBody> typing

suggestions.assertion: Query includes condition builder (eq) for fetching by ID
suggestions.reason: The without_skill version uses LoadStateAggregateClient.load(id) which bypasses the QueryDSL condition builder. While functionally correct, it doesn't demonstrate use of the condition builders like eq() or aggregateId().

eval_feedback.overall: The skill version demonstrates proper use of condition builders (aggregateId) while the without_skill version uses a simpler but less sophisticated approach.
